title: Port Phase 5: dev tooling, embedded build, deploy, docs
description: Tasks 13-16. justfile, release build w/ rust-embed, .env.example, systemd unit, data carryover, CLAUDE.md/README updates.

title: Port Phase 6: end-to-end verification
description: Tasks 17-18. Playwright login+parity smoke against release binary, final gates, final PR working->main.

title: Port Phase 4: frontend move + invoke->fetch + auth UI
description: Tasks 10-12. Move SvelteKit to frontend/, shared fetch client, rewrite 13 api wrappers, (app) route group + guard + /login page.

title: Port Phase 3: API routes (all 79 endpoints)
description: Tasks 6-9. Cameras exemplar + all CRUD modules + import (server-side Anthropic key) + auth-guard regression test. Command->REST.
close_reason: Phase 3 merged via PR; 71 endpoints (not 79), all reviews + phase review passed

title: Port Phase 2: single-password auth
description: Tasks 4-5. argon2 hashing + hash-password CLI; tower-sessions session layer; login/logout/me handlers; RequireAuth extractor (open LAN-trust mode when no hash).

title: Port Phase 1: workspace restructure (entity/migration/binary crates)
description: Tasks 1-3 of the port plan. Extract entity crate, move migration crate, create root axum binary skeleton (health + SPA serve), drop Tauri. End state: cargo build passes, server serves /api/health.

title: Replace dead undecided status chevrons with a development-path decision chevron
description: The undecided flow renders two non-interactive '·' placeholder divs between Shot and Scanned, while the real lab/self choice lives in a disconnected Development section (+Lab/+Self). Replace the dead dots with ONE actionable accent-tinted 'Develop ⌄' chevron that opens a 2-item popover (Lab / Self-Home). Choosing a path sets devAutoPrompt, opening the corresponding dev dialog; on save the backend auto-syncs status and the bar re-renders to labFlow/selfFlow via existing getFlowForPath. Scanned/Archived stay visible but muted as path-agnostic endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: Undecided bar shows a single live 'Develop' chevron (no dead dots); clicking opens Lab/Self menu; choosing opens the right dev dialog; after creating a dev record the bar re-renders to the chosen path's flow; chevron is a real button with aria-haspopup/expanded

title: Capture shooting→shot transition date via Mark-as-Shot nudge
description: When a roll transitions to 'shot', date_finished is silently dropped — updateStatus('shot') writes only status. The date_finished field already exists end-to-end but is manual-only (Edit dialog). Add an editable date field (prefilled with today) to the existing 'Roll complete → Mark as Shot' nudge so the finish date is captured + confirmable in one gesture. Also stamp date_finished=today when entering 'shot' via the chevron if still empty, so the date is never lost.
acceptance_criteria: Nudge shows a date field prefilled with today; Mark as Shot writes date_finished; chevron-driven shot transition stamps today if empty; existing 'Finished {date}' header reflects it

title: Fix pre-existing svelte-check type errors (30)
description: The frontend had 31 svelte-check errors + 1 warning before the port (present on the original main). Mostly 'type never' inference in search/stats and enum-string narrowing in film-stocks/rolls/import, plus process global in vite.config. Not caused by the port. Clean these up so 'bun run check' / CI is green.
notes:
Fixed all 30 svelte-check errors. Three root-cause categories:

1. 'never' inference (search/stats): `let x: T | null = $state(null)` puts the annotation on the variable but the rune's own generic infers `null`, so the null-guard narrowed to `never`. Fixed with `$state<T | null>(null)`.

2. Enum-string narrowing (cameras, film-stocks, import, rolls): <Select> binds string state (DOM reality — Select.value is $bindable('')), but DTO fields want enums (CameraFormat/CameraType/MaintenanceType/FilmFormat/FilmStockType/RollStatus/PushPull). Cast at the one-way API boundary (`x as Enum`); backend DeriveActiveEnum is the runtime validator.

3. Closure narrowing loss + ordering (rolls/[id], cameras/[id]): `roll?.x` guard narrowing is discarded inside nested .find() arrows on a reassignable let — re-apply `?.` inside the callback. Also moved frameProgress $derived above its use, and `x = r ?? undefined` for getRoll/getCamera returning T|null into a T|undefined state.

4. DevStageInsert mismatch (DevelopmentSection): nested stages in a self-dev create/update don't carry development_self_id (assigned server-side; backend StageDto omits it). Added NestedStageInsert = Omit<DevStageInsert,'development_self_id'> in api/development.ts.

Verified: bun run check = 0 errors / 0 warnings; bun run build succeeds.
close_reason: All 30 svelte-check errors fixed; bun run check and bun run build both green.

title: Transactional delete-of-missing returns 422 not 404 (shots, lab/self dev)
description: The 3 transactional delete handlers — shots.rs::delete_one, development.rs::delete_lab_dev, development.rs::delete_self_dev — look up the row inside the txn closure with .ok_or_else(|| DbErr::Custom(format!("X {id} not found"))). That DbErr propagates out and is mapped via friendly_err -> AppError::UnprocessableEntity, so DELETE of an already-removed id returns HTTP 422 VALIDATION_ERROR with a misleading 'Could not save X: X {id} not found' message instead of 404 NOT_FOUND. Every non-transactional handler (swept to OptionExt::or_404 in kammerz-19v) correctly returns 404 for the same condition. Pre-existing (predates kammerz-19v); deliberately scoped out of that sweep because the lookup runs inside a DbErr-typed txn closure. Deeper fix: add a From<DbErr> arm classifying DbErr::RecordNotFound as AppError::NotFound plus a DbErr-side or_404 helper, unifying all ~88 sites.
notes: Found by /code-review on kammerz-19v. Real but minor: stale-id double-delete from the frontend gets 422+'could not save' instead of 404. Fix touches error.rs error classification, outside the kammerz-19v diff.

title: Roll detail: reference lists refetched on every load() call
description: Code-review (efficiency, follow-up to kammerz-0e5): frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/[id]/+page.svelte load() re-fetches 5 reference lists (listCameras, listFilmStocks, listLenses, listLabs, listLensMounts) on EVERY call. load() is the sole refresh path — invoked after every shot create/edit/delete and via DevelopmentSection onchange={load} — so each mutation re-pulls all reference data even though it rarely changes. kammerz-0e5 collapsed the roll-scoped round-trips into the composite /detail endpoint but intentionally left these 5 as-is. Caveat: labs and lenses can be created inline on this page, so a naive 'fetch once' cache would go stale after inline creation — the fix must refresh those caches (or just the affected list) after an inline create. Options: (a) split load() into loadRefData() [once on mount] + loadRollData() [on mutation], invalidating ref caches only after inline creates; (b) fold reference data into a composite bootstrap. Pre-existing from the Tauri version; behavior-preserving optimization.
close_reason: Split roll-detail load() into loadRefData() (5 reference lists, once on mount) + loadRollData() (composite /detail, on every mutation). The issue's inline-create caveat was found not to apply — lens/lab dropdowns are read-only Selects, no inline creation on this page — so no cache invalidation needed. Verified: svelte-check 0/0, prod build, and Playwright network trace (ref lists fetched once, only /detail re-fires after a roll-edit mutation; ref data reloads on SPA remount).

title: Change server default port from 3001 to 3002
description: Make the axum server's default listen port 3002 (currently 3001). Update every place the default appears so dev + docs stay consistent: src/main.rs (PORT fallback unwrap_or(3001)); frontend/vite.config.ts (the /api proxy target's 'Number(process.env.PORT) || 3001'); justfile (dev/comments referencing :3001); .env.example (PORT=3001); README quickstart; CLAUDE.md (Commands/Architecture mention :3001). The PORT env var still overrides. After the change, re-run the embedded smoke + Playwright e2e (E2E_BASE/PORT) against the new default.
close_reason: Changed default server port 3001→3002 across main.rs, vite/playwright/smoke configs, justfile, .env.example, README, CLAUDE.md. Verified: cargo build, bun run check, runtime smoke (default binds 3002, PORT override intact).

title: Auth hardening: login rate-limit + evaluate DB pool size
description: Code-review (security/efficiency, non-blocking): (1) POST /api/auth/login has no rate limiting — a shared-password brute-force over LAN/VPN is bounded only by argon2 cost; consider a tower governor/backoff. (2) db.rs uses a max=min=1 SQLite pool (intentional for FK-during-migration determinism + in-memory tests), which serializes all requests; if concurrency ever matters, evaluate a small read pool with pragmas via after_connect. Both are accepted tradeoffs for a single-user LAN app today.

title: Roll detail page: use composite getRollDetail() endpoint
description: Code-review (efficiency): frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/[id]/+page.svelte fires 9 parallel + 2 sequential API calls in load(), but the composite GET /api/rolls/{id}/detail (getRollDetail() wrapper) was built to collapse the roll/shots/shot-lens/lab-dev/self-dev/stages round-trips into one and currently has 0 call sites (dead). Rewire the page to use it. Pre-existing from the Tauri version; behavior-affecting so needs testing.
close_reason: Rewired roll detail page load() to use composite getRollDetail() endpoint, collapsing 6 roll-scoped round-trips (roll/shots/shot-lens/lab-dev/self-dev/stages) into one. 5 reference-list lookups remain parallel. Verified: svelte-check 0 errors, prod build ok, browser render of populated roll + 404 not-found path both correct.

title: Reduce route-handler boilerplate (From<DbErr>, now_string, find_or_404)
description: Code-review findings: 53 read handlers use '.map_err(|e| AppError::Internal(e.to_string()))' but error.rs already has 'impl From<DbErr> for AppError' producing exactly that — use bare '?'. ~22 copies of chrono::Utc::now().format("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S").to_string() — add patch::now_string(). 10 update handlers repeat 'fetch-or-404' — add a find_or_404 helper. All behavior-preserving cleanups deferred from the port; do as one mechanical sweep with tests.
close_reason: Mechanical behavior-preserving sweep: 53 map_err(Internal) → bare ? (via existing From<DbErr>), 22 timestamp literals → patch::now_string(), 10 fetch-or-404 → OptionExt::or_404. cargo build + 26 tests + frontend build + svelte-check all green.

title: Add logout UI control to Sidebar
description: auth.svelte.ts store has logout() but no UI wires it. Add a sign-out control to the Sidebar that calls auth.logout() and redirects to /login. Deferred from Phase 4 of the web port.

title: Add 'Copy chemistry from previous roll' to self-development dialog
description: Batch home dev sessions use identical chemistry across multiple rolls — currently every roll requires re-entering developer, fixer, dilutions, temps, stages, etc. Add a dropdown of recent self-dev rolls; selecting one copies its chemistry fields and stage list into the current dialog. Source: IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md 'Future Feature Ideas → Self-Development UX Improvements'.
design: Sourcing this from DevelopmentService::list_recent_self_dev (new helper) rather than reusing existing list endpoints — only need a few fields, want fast dropdown population. Returns roll_id, roll display name, dev_date for the picker.
acceptance_criteria: Dropdown in self-dev dialog lists the last N rolls with a self-dev record (probably 10, sorted desc by date). Selecting a roll copies: developer, fixer, stop, wash agents, dilutions, temperatures, agitation notes, and the entire stage list. User can edit any field before saving. Original roll's data is unmodified.

title: Add stage preset templates to self-development dialog
description: Self-dev dialog starts with empty stages every time, forcing users to retype the same B&W workflow (pre-soak → developer → stop → fixer → wash → wetting agent). Offer one-click presets to populate stages. Source: IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md 'Future Feature Ideas → Self-Development UX Improvements'.
acceptance_criteria: Preset dropdown in self-dev dialog. At least one B&W preset shipped (pre-soak, developer, stop, fixer, wash, wetting). Selecting a preset populates the stage list with sensible default durations. Stages remain editable after applying preset. Optional: presets for C-41 / E-6.

title: Add holder_id field to shots for large-format tracking (Film Holder Tier 2)
description: Sheet film loaded into large-format holders (4x5+) currently has no structured tracking — users store holder IDs in free-text notes. Tier 2 from IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md 'Future Feature Ideas → Film Holder Support': add an optional holder_id TEXT column + text input. Smallest useful step. Tier 3 (full holder entity) deferred until Tier 2 proves the need.
acceptance_criteria: New migration adds nullable holder_id TEXT to shots table. Shot entity + DTOs updated. Shot entry dialog has a text input for holder ID. Roll detail page displays holder ID alongside the frame. Search service includes the field. No frame-number changes — auto-suggest remains numeric-only.
notes: suggest_next_frame() in shot_service.rs parses frames as i32 — non-numeric holder-side IDs like '3B' are silently skipped for auto-suggest. Acceptable for Tier 2. If users start typing structured holder-side notation, revisit.

title: Manual QA smoke pass after time away
description: Phase 3e of IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md left 4 manual smoke-test items unchecked when the SeaORM migration shipped. They were likely validated implicitly by 6+ weeks of subsequent development on top of those code paths, but a deliberate pass before the next major feature lands would confirm. After verification, check the boxes in IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md so the doc stops claiming things are unverified.
acceptance_criteria: Run `bun run tauri dev` and verify: (1) All CRUD operations work end-to-end (create/edit/delete a camera, lens, roll, shot); (2) ComboInput autocomplete is populated (brands, vendors, lens systems on the relevant dialogs); (3) Film stock format-aware ordering still works on New Roll page (e.g., 120 stocks for medium-format camera); (4) Dashboard stats and 'Needs Attention' alerts display correctly. Update IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md Phase 3e section to mark verified items.

title: Print/share roll summary view
description: Generate a printable / shareable summary for a single roll — metadata + shot list — useful for posting alongside scans or archiving with the physical negatives.
design: Use CSS @media print for the layout. Single full-width column, mono font for the shot table, no app chrome. Date/cost summary at the top. If image attachments exist (depends on attachments feature), inline thumbnails at small print sizes.
acceptance_criteria: Roll detail page has a 'Print summary' action. Opens a print-friendly view. Layout includes: roll metadata (camera, film stock, dates), dev info (lab or self chemistry/stages), shot table (frame, lens, aperture/shutter, location, notes). Browser print dialog produces a clean PDF.

title: Attach scanned images to rolls and shots
description: Link scanned negative/print images to a roll or individual frame so the app becomes the source of truth for both metadata AND the visuals. Store images in the app data directory, reference via a new attachments table.
design: Filesystem storage with path references (new attachments table: id, parent_type, parent_id, file_path, kind, created_at) — NOT BLOBs in SQLite (DB bloat, backup pain). Use a content-hash subdir scheme (first 2 chars of hash) to avoid huge flat dirs. Image export should copy referenced files into the export bundle, not just paths.
acceptance_criteria: Add attachment from roll detail and shot dialog. Thumbnail rendered in shot list. Click to view full-size in a lightbox. Files stored under platform app-data dir (e.g., ~/Library/Application Support/com.kammerz.app/attachments/ on macOS). Drag-and-drop nice-to-have.

title: Add bulk operations on rolls (and other entities)
description: Select multiple items on a list page and apply one action to all of them — e.g., archive a batch of rolls, mark several as sent to lab. Reduces friction when catching up on data entry after a shooting trip.
design: Start with rolls only — the highest-value list. Cameras/lenses can come later. Reuse existing ConfirmDialog component. Action bar appears in PageHeader when selection > 0.
acceptance_criteria: Multi-select UI on rolls list page (checkbox column + 'select all' header). Bulk actions for rolls: status change, archive, delete. Confirmation dialog shows affected count. Backend uses a transaction so all-or-nothing.

title: Add CSV/JSON import for catalog data
description:
Restore from a JSON export (round-trip), or bulk-load from CSVs for migrating from spreadsheets / other apps. Useful when moving between installs.

Depends on the JSON export feature — export defines the schema.
design: Conflict resolution should default to 'skip if exists' to avoid surprises; expose 'overwrite' as an explicit option. Validate against schema version on JSON import — reject newer versions, migrate older versions if practical.
acceptance_criteria: JSON import accepts files produced by the export feature. CSV import supports at least cameras, lenses, film stocks (per-table). Conflict handling defined (skip duplicates by natural key vs. overwrite). Transactional — partial failure rolls back the whole import.

title: Add full-catalog JSON export
description:
Export every table to a single JSON file so users can back up their catalog or move it between machines. Defines the schema that the import feature consumes — build this first.

Trigger from a Settings/Tools page, write to user-chosen path via Tauri's file picker.
design: Single-file JSON over per-table CSVs — preserves cross-table FKs (rolls→camera_id, etc.) cleanly. Use a stable schema-version field so import can branch on it. Run inside a SeaORM transaction to ensure consistent snapshot.
acceptance_criteria: User triggers export from a Settings/Tools page; file picker writes a JSON file. All 13 tables included. Top-level kammerz_export_version field for forward compatibility. Re-importing the file produces an equivalent catalog.

title: Phase 7: Data Portability & Polish
description: Final unimplemented phase from IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md. Covers export, import, bulk operations, media attachments, and print/share. Each child issue is a distinct feature under this epic.
notes: Source: IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md "Phase 7: Data Portability & Polish". Children should depend on this epic so closing the last child auto-suggests closing the epic.
